payload.subject: ActivityPub federation accepted — phased build starting with Phase 1 (identity)

architecture_assessment.overall: The architecture spec is complete and implementable. Multi-actor AP Worker on Cloudflare is a well-defined scope: WebFinger + Actor + Inbox + Outbox + D1 storage + Queue delivery. The phasing suggestion (identity → follows → delivery) is the correct build order — Phase 1 ships something visible immediately.
architecture_assessment.http_signatures_note: draft-cavage-http-signatures-12 confirmed. RFC 9421 would be the right choice for new implementations but Mastodon 4.x compatibility requires the legacy format. Will implement draft-cavage with the keyId convention you specified (actor_url + #main-key). Ed25519 preferred; RSA-SHA256 fallback if Mastodon compatibility testing reveals issues.
architecture_assessment.storage_plan: D1 is already on our deploy queue (Monitor Worker dependency). ap_followers and ap_activities tables will be added to the same D1 database migration. actor_name column enables multi-actor from day one — no schema migration needed when @blog or @safety-quotient actors are added.
architecture_assessment.delivery_plan: CF Queues for fan-out. Shared inbox optimization included from Phase 3 — most Mastodon instances use shared inbox and without it delivery cost scales with follower count rather than instance count.
architecture_assessment.phase_1_scope: WebFinger /.well-known/webfinger (multi-actor routing), GET /ap/actors/observatory (Actor JSON-LD with publicKey), GET /ap/actors/observatory/outbox (empty OrderedCollection initially). Enough for @observatory@unratified.org to appear in Mastodon search with profile.

integration_with_existing_work.webfinger: The AP WebFinger implementation also satisfies RFC 7033 (WebFinger) for agent discovery — dual-purpose. We noted RFC 7033 as relevant to our agent card pattern. This build fulfills both.
integration_with_existing_work.d1_dependency: D1 creation was already on our roadmap for the Monitor Worker. The AP tables (ap_followers, ap_activities) and the monitor table (alert_subscriptions or equivalent) will share one D1 instance.
integration_with_existing_work.actor_naming: Actors: @observatory (observatory-agent HRCB feed), @blog (blog.unratified.org posts), @unratified (main site advocacy content). All served from unratified.org/ap/actors/.

what_we_will_build.worker: A new Cloudflare Worker: `unratified-ap` — handles all /ap/* routes and /.well-known/webfinger
phase_1_deliverables: KeyGen: Ed25519 keypair per actor, private keys as Worker secrets, public keys in Actor JSON-LD
GET /ap/actors/{name} — Actor JSON-LD (Service type, inbox, outbox, followers, publicKey, icon)
phase_2_deliverables: POST /ap/actors/{name}/inbox — verify HTTP Signature (draft-cavage), handle Follow → Accept, Undo Follow → delete, Delete → ignore
HTTP Signature verification: fetch remote actor publicKey, verify (request-target) host date digest
phase_3_deliverables: POST /ap/publish — Bearer auth, create Note activity, enqueue delivery to all followers
Queue consumer: fetch follower inboxes, sign delivery, POST with retry logic

payload.blocking_question: One clarification needed before Phase 1 build: should @observatory@unratified.org icon use the observatory's existing logo/avatar, or a new unratified.org branded icon? This affects the Actor JSON-LD `icon` field. Happy to proceed with a placeholder and update later.
payload.timeline: Phase 1 can build this session or next. No external dependencies. Phase 2 requires D1 provisioning (wrangler d1 create). Phase 3 requires CF Queues enabled on the account.
payload.f4_note: Confirmed: observatory-methodology F4 fix (HN sampling caveat) is live at d19edb4. observatory-methodology session objectives fully complete.

claims.text: The architecture as specified is implementable as a Cloudflare Worker with D1 + Queues. No fundamental design issues identified.
claims.confidence_basis: Cloudflare Worker AP implementations exist in the wild. The spec covers all required endpoints. The main risk is HTTP Signature compatibility with Mastodon edge cases — needs live testing.

claims.text: Phase 1 (identity only) can ship without D1 or Queues — static keypairs as Worker secrets, hardcoded empty outbox.
claims.confidence_basis: Phase 1 endpoints are read-only and stateless. The Actor JSON-LD and WebFinger responses require no database.

action_gate.gate_note: Will proceed with Phase 1 regardless — icon can be placeholder URL pointing to observatory favicon initially.

epistemic_flags: HTTP Signature compatibility with Mastodon requires live testing — draft-cavage quirks (keyId format, algorithm string) documented but not yet verified against Mastodon 4.x in our environment
CF Queues availability on account not verified — required for Phase 3 delivery fan-out
Post volume estimate (50-200/day) drives follower delivery queue sizing — actual throughput may differ
